reachability_artifacts: built sworn control pause invocation recording operation ID, unchanged pre-loop status revision, claimed operation event, new loop-produced revision, output, and exit code
serialized MCP JSON-RPC patch-retry and stale-revision responses plus a TUI key-driven pause flow, reconciled to the same command/event rows

effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; adapter command taxonomy and transport error mappings require implementer confirmation before design review.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can trust a recoverable autonomous release loop, receive durable attention pages, and monitor or safely control it from a mobile web board.

positive_scenarios: CLI, MCP, and TUI submit the same pause command and observe the same operation lifecycle.
All three surfaces show the same authoritative source and revision.
negative_scenarios: A stale command is visibly rejected on every surface.
No runtime adapter directly rewrites status or starts a nested loop.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Adapter parity removes split-brain controls and creates the trustworthy base required by web/mobile operations.

design_decisions.choice: Expose controls through a dedicated sworn control command and transport adapters over the same service.
design_decisions.rationale: One command contract provides scriptable reachability and prevents each UI from becoming an application core.

design_decisions.choice: Keep adapters read-after-command rather than optimistically mutating displayed Baton state.
options: query authoritative operation and state snapshots
design_decisions.rationale: Only the loop's accepted compare-and-write revision is authoritative.
